title: Job Openings at BFree
content:
BFREE - We are a young, innovative and Nigeria’s foremost ethical debt collection startup. If you want to have fun while working in a fun environment, earn very well when you perform like a rock-star, and be part of a company where your voice matters, then Bfree is the place for you. Because we hate status-quo and we hate mediocre - we founded BFREE to revolutionize debt recovery in Nigeria

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Investment Associate

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

            
    




	The Investment Associate is a core analytical role sitting at the intersection of portfolio performance, collections strategy, and investment decision-making. You will own the continuous analysis of BFree's debt portfolios, diagnosing performance, identifying the levers that drive collections outcomes, and translating data into actionable recommendations that the operations, collections, and investment teams can execute against.
	This is not a passive reporting role.
	We expect the Investment Associate to bring intellectual rigour, intellectual curiosity, and a genuine drive to understand why portfolios perform the way they do and what can be done about it.


Key Responsibilities

Portfolio Performance Analysis


	Maintain portfolio-level and cohort-level performance models across BFree's loan books
	Track key metrics (collection rates, cure rates, roll rates, IRR vs. underwriting) across portfolio vintages, products, and counterparties
	Produce regular performance reporting for internal and external stakeholders
	Identify performance outliers positive and negative and lead root-cause investigations


Collections Strategy & Optimisation


	Work closely with operations and collections leadership to design and evaluate interventions: channel mix, contact cadence, settlement strategies, agent allocation
	Design and analyse A/B tests and operational experiments to establish causal evidence for what drives collections uplift
	Model the expected impact of proposed strategy changes before deployment, and track realised vs. projected outcomes post-deployment.


Data & Tooling


	Query and manipulate large datasets using SQL; build and maintain analytical models in Python, R, or Excel
	Work with the data engineering team to define analytical requirements, flag data quality issues, and improve the analytical infrastructure
	Develop reusable dashboards and automated reporting where appropriate.


Requirements
Education:


	MBA preferred, particularly from a programme with a strong finance, strategy, or analytics track
	Bachelor's or Master's Degree in a quantitative discipline (Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, Finance, Computer Science) also strongly considered.


Experience:


	2–4 years of experience in a quantitatively intensive role: consumer lending analytics, credit risk, structured finance, management consulting, investment banking, or distressed debt
	Prior exposure to debt collections, credit portfolio management, or NPL markets is a  significant plus
	Experience operating in African financial markets preferred.


Skills & Competencies:


	Exceptional quantitative and logical reasoning: you can build a model from scratch, stress-test your own assumptions, and know when an answer doesn't pass the smell test
	Proficiency in SQL (required); Python or R for statistical analysis (required);
	Excel/Google Sheets financial modelling (required)
	Ability to design and interpret experiments: you understand statistical significance, selection bias, and the limits of observational data
	Strong written and verbal communication: able to translate complex analysis into clear, decision-oriented narratives for non-technical audiences
	Detail-oriented and self-directed: comfortable managing multiple work-streams with limited oversight in a fast-moving environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Chief of Staff

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	The Chief of Staff sits at the center of BFREE’s operations, working directly alongside the founders and leadership team across every vertical of the business. You are not a scheduler or a note-taker.
	You are an operator: someone who takes a problem, structures it, and drives it to a conclusion with limited hand-holding and high standards.
	The portfolio is deliberately broad and will shift with the company’s priorities: one week you might be preparing investor materials and coordinating a data room; the next, you could be on the ground in a new market scoping a launch or sitting in a meeting with a regulator.
	You will be exposed to every dimension of a fast-growing African fintech including strategy, operations, commercial, fundraising, and expansion earlier than almost any other role at your stage would offer.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the founders across strategic priorities including structuring problems, building analyses, and following through on execution so nothing falls through the cracks.
	Own discrete special projects end to end: market entry assessments, operational deep-dives, partnership scoping, process improvement initiatives.
	Contribute to fundraising efforts, building presentations and data packs, researching investors, coordinating logistics, preparing briefing notes ahead of meetings.
	Support BFREE’s pan-African expansion by conducting on-the-ground research, building local relationships, and helping stand up new market operations.
	Step into fires: when something breaks or needs urgent attention, you diagnose quickly, bring structure, and help the team move through it.
	Attend meetings with external stakeholders; banking partners, regulators, shareholders.
	Prepared, attentive, and ready to support follow-through on whatever comes out of them.
	Produce board-quality outputs: concise memos, sharp slide decks, structured analyses that allow leadership to make fast, well-informed decisions.
	Track cross-functional workstreams, flag blockers early, and keep the organisation aligned on what matters most.
	Travel across the continent regularly to support expansion, stakeholder engagement, and wherever the work takes you.


Requirements


	At least 2 years of experience in a front-office role at a Tier 1 management consulting firm (McKinsey, BCG, Bain, or equivalent), a Tier 1 investment bank, or a Venture Capital / Private Equity firm. You have been trained to think rigorously and work at pace.
	Knowledge of GenAI, prompt coding will be an added advantage.
	Strong analytical and structured thinking skills. You can break down a messy problem, build a clean model or framework around it, and communicate your findings clearly.
	High ownership and follow-through: you do not hand problems back, you do not need constant direction, and you hold yourself accountable for outcomes rather than activities.
	Genuine comfort with ambiguity. The brief will shift, the priorities will change, and you will need to adapt without losing momentum.
	The maturity to engage with senior stakeholders including founders, board members, institutional investors, regulators, professionally and without needing to be managed.
	A willingness to travel extensively across Sub-Saharan Africa as a regular part of the role.
	Fluency in English. French, Arabic or Swahili is an advantage.
	An interest in or existing connection to the African tech and financial services landscape.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Treasury Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	The Treasury Manager owns BFREE’s financial risk and liquidity posture end to end. You will build and run the treasury function from the ground up: the policies, the tools, the controls, and the relationships.
	You will report directly to the CFO and work alongside the founders, with visibility into every dimension of the business - portfolio performance, capital allocation, debt structuring, and expansion.
	This is not a role for someone who wants to inherit a mature function.
	There is no playbook yet.
	We are early in our treasury management system journey, our FX exposures are growing as we add markets, and the work of turning informal practices into institutional-grade infrastructure is largely ahead of us.
	The right person is energised by that, not unsettled by it.
	Currency and liquidity are two of the dominant risks at BFREE. On the liquidity side, we are an active acquirer of distressed portfolios across multiple markets, and ensuring the business is always funded - with the right cash in the right currency in the right jurisdiction at the right time - is a constant operational discipline. On the currency side, we finance multi-currency portfolio acquisitions, often with hard currency debt facilities, and managing the mismatch between the currency of funding and the currency of deployment is a core part of the role. The primary
	hedging mandate is to protect BFREE’s ability to service and repay hard currency obligations as local currencies move. Where instruments and market conditions allow, preserving portfolio yield is an additional objective. Both mandates require someone who understands how things actually work in African markets - from banking infrastructure and capital controls to deposit-backed structures, SBLCs, NDFs, and short-dated forwards - and has the relationships and structuring experience to execute across multiple jurisdictions simultaneously.


Key Responsibilities
Liquidity & Cash Management:


	Own daily cash positioning across all entities and jurisdictions, ensuring BFREE is always funded and never surprised.
	Forecast short- and medium-term liquidity needs, coordinating with portfolio acquisition, operations, and finance teams.
	Manage bank and counterparty relationships; set and enforce counterparty limits to manage concentration risk across the banking partners we rely on.
	Define minimum liquidity buffers, escalation protocols, and an investment policy for idle cash - T-bills, money market funds, short-duration instruments - within approved risk parameters.


Foreign Exchange Management:


	Monitor and manage BFREE’s multi-currency exposure across NGN, KES, GHS, USD, and EUR on an ongoing basis.
	Stay ahead of capital controls, FX regulations, and repatriation rules in each operating market; manage intercompany flows and cross-border transfers accordingly.
	Own FX reporting: open positions, exposure by entity, limit utilisation, and regulatory compliance - reported to the CFO on a regular cadence.
	Manage relationships with FX counterparties and banking desks across markets.


Hedging & Risk Mitigation:


	Design and own BFREE’s hedging strategy: the primary objective is protecting BFREE’s ability to service and repay hard currency debt facilities as local currencies depreciate; the secondary objective is preserving portfolio yield where instruments and market conditions allow.
	Structure and execute deposit-backed hedges - placing USD collateral with a banking counterparty and drawing a matched local currency loan against it - to synthetically convert hard-currency funding into local currency exposure across large facilities.
	Arrange and manage Standby Letters of Credit (SBLCs) as credit support instruments, negotiating terms with issuing and beneficiary banks to support local currency borrowing.
	Execute and roll short-dated FX forwards and non-deliverable forwards (NDFs) - particularly for NGN where physical settlement is constrained - managing rollover risk and timing across multiple positions.
	Review and critically assess all hedging contracts and facility documentation before execution - in close coordination with the CFO - ensuring economic terms, collateral obligations, and exit provisions are fully understood and acceptable.
	Monitor hedge coverage continuously against drawdown schedules and portfolio performance; rebalance as conditions evolve.
	Advise the CFO and founders on hedging cost vs. exposure trade-offs as new markets and facilities are added, with a clear view on what is achievable within the constraints of each local market.


Debt & Funding Management:


	Manage relationships with lenders, maintaining compliance with all covenants, reporting requirements, and drawdown conditions.
	Support fundraising processes by preparing treasury-related materials: debt term sheets, waterfall models, covenant compliance packs.
	Contribute to the structuring of new credit facilities and ensure funding is aligned with portfolio acquisition timelines and market expansion plans.
	Coordinate intercompany lending and cross-border capital deployment across BFREE’s operating entities.
	Work closely with the CFO and finance team to optimise the funding and entity structure for tax efficiency - including intercompany pricing, capital deployment routes, and the location of hedging instruments - ensuring treasury decisions actively support the group’s overall tax position.


Risk Governance & Controls:


	Author and maintain BFREE’s treasury policy framework: FX, hedging, liquidity, counterparty, and investment policy.
	Build and own the treasury risk dashboard - KPIs, limits, breaches, and trends - reported to the CFO and Board on a regular cadence.
	Ensure all treasury activity is compliant with local and international regulatory requirements across operating markets.
	Work with external auditors and regulators as required.


Systems, Automation & Process


	Lead BFREE’s TMS build-out: evaluate tools, make the case for investment, and implement the right solution for our current scale and trajectory.
	Use prompt engineering and AI tools to automate reporting, reconciliation, and process-heavy workflows - you should be able to do this yourself, without waiting for an engineer.
	Drive automation of cash reporting, exposure tracking, and covenant monitoring so the function scales without headcount scaling at the same rate.
	Partner with the CFO, finance, legal, tax, and operations in close coordination on all material treasury decisions - hedging structures, funding arrangements, entity-level cash management - so that treasury is integrated into the business rather than operating in isolation.


Requirements


	At least 5 years of treasury experience in a financial institution, corporate treasury function, or an African fintech / fund manager operating across multiple markets.
	Proven, hands-on hedging experience in African currency markets: you have structured and executed deposit-backed hedges, SBLCs, FX forwards, and NDFs - you understand the constraints of NGN, KES, and GHS and know how to work within them.
	Experience reviewing and negotiating hedging and facility contracts, with the rigour to scrutinise terms before committing.
	Hands-on experience with debt instruments, credit facility management, and covenant compliance.
	Strong quantitative and modelling skills. You can build a cash flow model, a hedging cost analysis, or a counterparty exposure report without waiting for someone else to do it.
	Familiarity with treasury management systems and financial data tools. Comfort with Excel as a minimum; Python or SQL is an advantage.
	Comfort with AI tools and prompt engineering as a practical lever for automation - you use LLMs to eliminate repetitive work, not just to ask questions.
	High ownership and operational independence. You manage problems, you do not escalate them without a proposed solution.
	The judgement and communication skills to present treasury risk and hedging strategy clearly to a non-technical leadership team and Board.
	Experience working in or across Nigeria, Kenya, or Ghana, with a working knowledge of local regulatory environments, banking infrastructure, and capital control regimes.
	A professional qualification in finance or accounting (ACCA, ACT, or equivalent) is an advantage but not a requirement - demonstrated experience and track record matter more.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Customer Support Personnel Job at Daily Gist Telecoms International




        
    

        
        Daily Gist Telecoms International is a Telecom platform developed to provide customers great shopping experience and flexible payment options with opportunity to make extra income through our Social Commerce business model. On the Daily Gist platform, customers can shop using our web and mobile app with multiple payment options. We operate a world of digitally transformed airtime vending and bill payment. We help you get involved in the "train of profit" that telecommunication firms enjoy! You enjoy a share of profit on every top-up of your phones, internet data, cable TV subscription, and electricity bill payments.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Support Personnel
     

            
    



Job Locations: Lagos and Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements


	Minimum of OND, HND, or Bachelor's Degree.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Basic computer knowledge.
	Ability to work with minimal supervision.
	Good customer service and problem-solving skills.
	Previous customer support experience is an added advantage.


Salary
N50,000 - N100,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Full Name, Age, Qualification, Gender, Residential Address, and three Recent Photographs to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted and invited for an interview.
	Applications are open immediately.
	Don't miss this opportunity to become part of a fast-growing telecom brand committed to innovation, excellence, and empowerment.
	Apply today and take the next step in your career with Daily Gist Telecoms Int'l Ltd.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Assistant",
  "description": "Peridot Forte - Our client in the Information Technology Services industry is seeking an experienced candidates to support inventory management, logistics coordination, and service centre operations while ensuring efficient movement of parts, accurate stock control, and compliance with operational standards in the capacity below:

Job Position: Logistics Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Type: On-site

Job Summary


	The Logistics Assistant will be responsible for supporting day-to-day logistics, inventory control, and administrative operations within the service centre.
	The role involves maintaining accurate inventory records, coordinating shipments, managing documentation, and ensuring the smooth movement of parts and equipment to support service delivery and operational efficiency.


Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record incoming parts and equipment.
	Maintain accurate inventory records across inventory and CRM systems.
	Monitor stock levels and support inventory counts, audits, and reconciliations.
	Coordinate the movement of parts and equipment between service centre locations.
	Prepare shipment documentation and ensure proper packaging and dispatch of items.
	Track deliveries and follow up on delays to ensure service commitments are met.
	Manage the receipt, dispatch, and return of defective parts and equipment.
	Maintain accurate documentation for reporting, compliance, and audit purposes.
	Provide administrative and operational support to the service operations team.
	Ensure proper storage, organization, and security of inventory items.
	Support engineers and service personnel with timely allocation and issuance of parts.
	Ensure compliance with operational procedures and service-level agreements (SLAs).


Requirements


	Minimum of OND qualification in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	1&ndash;3 years&#39; experience in logistics, inventory management, warehouse operations, or a related role.
	Experience within an IT hardware, technical service centre, or technology-related environment is an added advantage.
	Proficiency in inventory management systems, CRM tools, and Microsoft Office applications.
	Good understanding of logistics, inventory control, and supply chain processes.
	Strong organizational, time-management, and record-keeping skills.
	Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively across teams.
	High attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: peridotforte@gmail.com using &quot;Logistics Assistant&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Logistics Assistant Job at Peridot Forte Solutions Consulting




        
    

        
        Peridot Forte - Our client in the Information Technology Services industry is seeking an experienced candidates to support inventory management, logistics coordination, and service centre operations while ensuring efficient movement of parts, accurate stock control, and compliance with operational standards in the capacity below:

Job Position: Logistics Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Type: On-site

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Logistics Assistant will be responsible for supporting day-to-day logistics, inventory control, and administrative operations within the service centre.
	The role involves maintaining accurate inventory records, coordinating shipments, managing documentation, and ensuring the smooth movement of parts and equipment to support service delivery and operational efficiency.


Responsibilities


	Receive, inspect, and record incoming parts and equipment.
	Maintain accurate inventory records across inventory and CRM systems.
	Monitor stock levels and support inventory counts, audits, and reconciliations.
	Coordinate the movement of parts and equipment between service centre locations.
	Prepare shipment documentation and ensure proper packaging and dispatch of items.
	Track deliveries and follow up on delays to ensure service commitments are met.
	Manage the receipt, dispatch, and return of defective parts and equipment.
	Maintain accurate documentation for reporting, compliance, and audit purposes.
	Provide administrative and operational support to the service operations team.
	Ensure proper storage, organization, and security of inventory items.
	Support engineers and service personnel with timely allocation and issuance of parts.
	Ensure compliance with operational procedures and service-level agreements (SLAs).


Requirements


	Minimum of OND qualification in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related field.
	1–3 years' experience in logistics, inventory management, warehouse operations, or a related role.
	Experience within an IT hardware, technical service centre, or technology-related environment is an added advantage.
	Proficiency in inventory management systems, CRM tools, and Microsoft Office applications.
	Good understanding of logistics, inventory control, and supply chain processes.
	Strong organizational, time-management, and record-keeping skills.
	Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively across teams.
	High attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected] using "Logistics Assistant" as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "City Manager",
  "description": "Max Drive - We are a team of passionate people and a leading technology-enabled company driven by a vision to solve the mobility challenges in Africa. We want to get people and goods to their destinations hitch-free. It is personal for us because we understand the frustration of epileptic transportation. We are making mobility safe, affordable, accessible, and sustainable through the deployment of high-performance technologies and operators.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: City Manager

Job Location: Abeokuta, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	We are looking for a reliable and highly organized City Manager who will be responsible for overseeing all aspects of the workspace operation (people, processes and systems) in order to ensure productivity targets are met.
	The ideal candidate will also be responsible for driving business process optimization; performance, and quality output at the location.


What You&rsquo;ll Do


	Direct all operational aspects, including growth( prospect to activation), operations(vehicle in and out), human resources (resource management), hub administration, and metrics for the profitability of the branch.
	Develop and implement branch metrics and reporting that provide critical insights regarding location performance, and ensure that issues are identified and appropriately addressed in a timely way
	Responsible for the overall administration and performance of the branch from prospecting to activation of champions, ensuring vehicle release, recovery and collection and fleet operations.
	Cooperate closely with the specific job matter experts( Growth; Operations:) in the identification and utilisation of opportunities, activities at the hub.
	Interface regularly and effectively with the operations and finance teams to continue improving on the systems and processes required to manage the hub portfolio.
	Plan, organise, direct and control specific day-to-day operations within the location/branch
	Ensure that the location is fully operational and processes are running smoothly.
	Implement and uphold MAX policies and procedures to drive compliance and maintain operational efficiency.
	Achieve specific targets and KPI&#39;s as agreed with Growth and Operations leadership.
	Responsible for assigning and managing resources to ensure efficient processes are utilized at the hub, thereby minimizing operational cost and maximizing operational quality.
	Analyze operational performance through direct observation, interpretation of reports and working collaboratively with others.
	Responsible for leading and developing a high performing team, ensuring consistently high levels of capability and engagement.
	Work with the Growth/Marketing Team to set clear prospects acquisition targets that are consistent with best practice and benchmarked against high-performing hubs
	Actively support &amp; contribute in critical metric management, Strategic alignment with business, and ensure key inputs are articulated in required forums to support superior service delivery.
	Lead and/or support external/internal audits, identify and control risk and support / drive incident investigations.
	Delivering superior Customer Experience through assertive leadership, coaching, guidance and staff motivation at the location.
	Support the development of a high-performance culture within teams by promoting continuous learning and focusing on meeting / exceeding needs / expectations.
	Operate within People Ops and Health, Safety and Environmental Policies and Guidelines and ensure employees work safely and comply
",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-30",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

City Manager Job at Max Drive




        
    

        
        Max Drive - We are a team of passionate people and a leading technology-enabled company driven by a vision to solve the mobility challenges in Africa. We want to get people and goods to their destinations hitch-free. It is personal for us because we understand the frustration of epileptic transportation. We are making mobility safe, affordable, accessible, and sustainable through the deployment of high-performance technologies and operators.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: City Manager

Job Location: Abeokuta, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

            
    




	We are looking for a reliable and highly organized City Manager who will be responsible for overseeing all aspects of the workspace operation (people, processes and systems) in order to ensure productivity targets are met.
	The ideal candidate will also be responsible for driving business process optimization; performance, and quality output at the location.


What You’ll Do


	Direct all operational aspects, including growth( prospect to activation), operations(vehicle in and out), human resources (resource management), hub administration, and metrics for the profitability of the branch.
	Develop and implement branch metrics and reporting that provide critical insights regarding location performance, and ensure that issues are identified and appropriately addressed in a timely way
	Responsible for the overall administration and performance of the branch from prospecting to activation of champions, ensuring vehicle release, recovery and collection and fleet operations.
	Cooperate closely with the specific job matter experts( Growth; Operations:) in the identification and utilisation of opportunities, activities at the hub.
	Interface regularly and effectively with the operations and finance teams to continue improving on the systems and processes required to manage the hub portfolio.
	Plan, organise, direct and control specific day-to-day operations within the location/branch
	Ensure that the location is fully operational and processes are running smoothly.
	Implement and uphold MAX policies and procedures to drive compliance and maintain operational efficiency.
	Achieve specific targets and KPI's as agreed with Growth and Operations leadership.
	Responsible for assigning and managing resources to ensure efficient processes are utilized at the hub, thereby minimizing operational cost and maximizing operational quality.
	Analyze operational performance through direct observation, interpretation of reports and working collaboratively with others.
	Responsible for leading and developing a high performing team, ensuring consistently high levels of capability and engagement.
	Work with the Growth/Marketing Team to set clear prospects acquisition targets that are consistent with best practice and benchmarked against high-performing hubs
	Actively support & contribute in critical metric management, Strategic alignment with business, and ensure key inputs are articulated in required forums to support superior service delivery.
	Lead and/or support external/internal audits, identify and control risk and support / drive incident investigations.
	Delivering superior Customer Experience through assertive leadership, coaching, guidance and staff motivation at the location.
	Support the development of a high-performance culture within teams by promoting continuous learning and focusing on meeting / exceeding needs / expectations.
	Operate within People Ops and Health, Safety and Environmental Policies and Guidelines and ensure employees work safely and comply
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Inventory Officer",
  "description": "Max Drive - We are a team of passionate people and a leading technology-enabled company driven by a vision to solve the mobility challenges in Africa. We want to get people and goods to their destinations hitch-free. It is personal for us because we understand the frustration of epileptic transportation. We are making mobility safe, affordable, accessible, and sustainable through the deployment of high-performance technologies and operators.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Ideal Inventory Officer will ensure proper inventory management, proper safekeeping of stock items, accuracy and effectiveness of stock reordering and stock level maintenance.
	Prepare and submit daily/weekly/monthly stock usage reports.


Key Responsibilities


	Receiving of new stocks and proper stock taking both physically and on the ERP platform and the relevant sheet
	Issuance of parts to refurbishment team and proper documentation of parts issued out
	Issuance of engine oil for maintenance, and documentation on ERP platform and the relevant sheet
	Monitor reorder level and determine reorder quantity of stock items by evaluating and analyzing internal customers request patterns.
	Weekly review of stock level &amp; initiation for replenishment on Odoo to maintain optimal and timely availability of stocks.
	Monitor stock movement and conduct regular stock counts to reconcile discrepancies.
	Raising of requests on Odoo for purchase of parts and other items used by the fleet/refurbishment team.
	Analyze and monitor request trends to identify fast moving and slow moving stock. This will help avoid tying down funds.
	Carry out any other task assigned.


Requirements


	A Degree in Finance, Accounting, Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or any relevant fields, Minimum of a Bsc/HND.
	Minimum Experience of 2 Years.
	Inventory Management and reporting skills
	Strong interpersonal and communication(written and oral) skills.
	Excellent understanding of Inventory Control principles and practices.
	Proficient in MS Office/Google Workspace.
	Good Maths SkillsCritical and analytical thinking.
	Accuracy and Attention to detail.
	Confidentiality and Thoroughness.

Inventory Officer Job at Max Drive




        
    

        
        Max Drive - We are a team of passionate people and a leading technology-enabled company driven by a vision to solve the mobility challenges in Africa. We want to get people and goods to their destinations hitch-free. It is personal for us because we understand the frustration of epileptic transportation. We are making mobility safe, affordable, accessible, and sustainable through the deployment of high-performance technologies and operators.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

            
    




	The Ideal Inventory Officer will ensure proper inventory management, proper safekeeping of stock items, accuracy and effectiveness of stock reordering and stock level maintenance.
	Prepare and submit daily/weekly/monthly stock usage reports.


Key Responsibilities


	Receiving of new stocks and proper stock taking both physically and on the ERP platform and the relevant sheet
	Issuance of parts to refurbishment team and proper documentation of parts issued out
	Issuance of engine oil for maintenance, and documentation on ERP platform and the relevant sheet
	Monitor reorder level and determine reorder quantity of stock items by evaluating and analyzing internal customers request patterns.
	Weekly review of stock level & initiation for replenishment on Odoo to maintain optimal and timely availability of stocks.
	Monitor stock movement and conduct regular stock counts to reconcile discrepancies.
	Raising of requests on Odoo for purchase of parts and other items used by the fleet/refurbishment team.
	Analyze and monitor request trends to identify fast moving and slow moving stock. This will help avoid tying down funds.
	Carry out any other task assigned.


Requirements


	A Degree in Finance, Accounting, Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or any relevant fields, Minimum of a Bsc/HND.
	Minimum Experience of 2 Years.
	Inventory Management and reporting skills
	Strong interpersonal and communication(written and oral) skills.
	Excellent understanding of Inventory Control principles and practices.
	Proficient in MS Office/Google Workspace.
	Good Maths SkillsCritical and analytical thinking.
	Accuracy and Attention to detail.
	Confidentiality and Thoroughness.
